canonical_definition: Based on the provided literature, pose estimation is characterized as an open-ended, cross-domain vision task. The single available definition describes it as the process of detecting an object's keypoints, such as those on a human or animal body, in order to determine its posture. This behavior is situated within the broader area of visual perception and is studied alongside other vision tasks like object localization and image generation. To operationalize and measure this behavior, the COCO dataset is used. The source material also specifies a technical approach, noting the adoption of "UniPose ... as the keypoint decoder" for the task.
papers: Lumen: Unleashing Versatile Vision-Centric Capabilities of Large Multimodal Models
VisionLLM v2: An End-to-End Generalist Multimodal Large Language Model for Hundreds of Vision-Language Tasks

definitions.text: The task of detecting the keypoints of an object, such as a human or animal body, to determine its posture.
definitions.paper: VisionLLM v2: An End-to-End Generalist Multimodal Large Language Model for Hundreds of Vision-Language Tasks
